questions.question: A soccer ball has a mass of $400\text{ g}$ and is moving at $25\text{ m/s}$. Calculate its momentum in SI units.
questions.explanation:
First, convert mass to kg: $400\text{ g} = 0.4\text{ kg}$. Then $p = 0.4 \times 25 = 10\text{ kg m/s}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using the mass in grams directly to get $10000$.

questions.question: A bus has a mass of $8000\text{ kg}$ and a momentum of $88000\text{ kg m/s}$. What is the speed of the bus?
questions.explanation:
Rearranging $p = mv$ gives $v = p/m$. $88000 / 8000 = 11\text{ m/s}$. 

**Common mistake:** Multiplying the numbers ($704$ million) instead of dividing.

questions.question: A car travelling at $12\text{ m/s}$ has a momentum of $14400\text{ kg m/s}$. Calculate the mass of the car.
questions.explanation:
Rearranging $p = mv$ gives $m = p/v$. $14400 / 12 = 1200\text{ kg}$. 

**Common mistake:** Multiplying velocity and momentum ($172800$).

questions.question: Two objects, A and B, are moving at the same velocity. Object A is more massive than object B. Which statement is correct?
options: Object A has more momentum than object B
Object B has more momentum than object A
They have the same momentum because their velocities are equal
Neither object has momentum because the force is not stated
questions.explanation:
Since momentum is the product of mass and velocity, if velocity is constant, the object with the greater mass will have the greater momentum. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking that same speed means same momentum regardless of mass.

questions.question: A car of mass $1000\text{ kg}$ is travelling at $18\text{ km/h}$. Calculate its momentum in $\text{kg m/s}$.
questions.explanation:
First convert $18\text{ km/h}$ to $\text{m/s}$: $18 \times (1000/3600) = 5\text{ m/s}$. Then $p = 1000 \times 5 = 5000\text{ kg m/s}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using the speed in $\text{km/h}$ directly ($18000$).
